story.iWant: a download button in the presentation header
story.soThat: I can access export options while viewing slides

tasks.task: Add CSS styles for download UI (~line 200 in viewer-template.html) .download-btn - Button styling matching existing controls .download-menu - Dropdown container (absolute positioned) .download-menu-item - Menu item with icon and text .download-menu.hidden - Hidden state
Add download button HTML (~line 443) Button with download SVG icon (down arrow with line) Button positioned between next-btn and close-btn onclick="toggleDownloadMenu()" title="Download (D)"
Add dropdown menu HTML (after download button) Menu container with three items Each item has icon + label Items call respective download functions
Add JavaScript functions (~line 825) toggleDownloadMenu() - Show/hide dropdown Click-outside handler to close menu 'D' keyboard shortcut handler

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Given I am viewing slides in presentation mode, when I look at the presentation header, then I see a download button next to the close button
When I click the download button, a dropdown menu opens with three options: Download Current Slide (PNG), Download All Slides (ZIP), Download as PDF (PDF)
Clicking outside the menu closes it
Pressing 'D' on keyboard opens/closes the menu

doc.title: Today - Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Add download menu HTML, CSS, JavaScript to viewer-template.html. CSS at ~line 200 for .download-btn, .download-menu, .download-menu-item. HTML at ~line 443 in .presentation-controls.

doc.title: Today - Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Code Style: Functions use camelCase, DOM queries use getElementById/querySelector, Event handlers inline on HTML elements, CSS variables for theming (var(--amp-lime), var(--amp-dark-alt)).

doc.title: Today - Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Download Button: Same size as close button (36x36px), download icon, same border/hover styling. Dropdown Menu: Dark background (var(--amp-dark-alt)), border 1px solid var(--amp-mid-gray), items with icons on left, hover state lime accent.

doc.title: Epic 12: Slide Download and Export
doc.snippet: First story in epic - no prerequisites. Modify viewer-template.html to add download dropdown menu in presentation header. Menu follows existing styling patterns (dark background, lime accent on hover).

doc.title: Slide Builder Architecture
doc.snippet: Frontend: Vanilla HTML/CSS/JavaScript, no framework, single HTML file with inline CSS and JavaScript, slide dimensions 1920x1080px (16:9).

file.reason: Button styling pattern to follow for download button - transparent background, border styling, hover states with lime accent
Close button styling to match - 36x36px dimensions, flexbox centering, same border/hover pattern
Container where download button will be added - flex layout with 16px gap
HTML location where download button should be inserted between next-btn and close-btn
Keyboard navigation pattern - switch statement on e.key, only handles in presentation mode. Add 'D' case here.

node.note: Not used for this story - browser-based rendering instead

browser.note: Will be added in Story 12.2 - not needed for UI story
Will be added in Story 12.4 - not needed for UI story
Will be added in Story 12.3 - not needed for UI story

constraints.constraint: Follow camelCase for function names (toggleDownloadMenu, not toggle_download_menu)
Use inline onclick handlers on HTML elements (onclick="toggleDownloadMenu()")
CSS variables for all colors (var(--amp-lime) not #d4e94c directly)
No external files - all CSS inline in style block, all JS in script block
Use existing transition patterns (transition: all 0.2s ease)
Must work with file:// protocol - no server required
Manual browser testing only - no automated test framework

interface.name: toggleDownloadMenu
interface.signature: function toggleDownloadMenu() - toggles .download-menu visibility via .hidden class

interface.name: downloadCurrentSlide
interface.signature: function downloadCurrentSlide() - placeholder for Story 12.2, logs message for now

interface.name: downloadAllSlidesPNG
interface.signature: function downloadAllSlidesPNG() - placeholder for Story 12.3, logs message for now

interface.name: downloadDeckPDF
interface.signature: function downloadDeckPDF() - placeholder for Story 12.4, logs message for now

tests.standards: Manual browser testing in Chrome, Safari, and Firefox. Open viewer index.html directly via file:// protocol. Test all UI interactions (click, keyboard) in presentation mode. Verify styling matches existing controls.

ideas.idea: Open viewer in presentation mode, visually verify download button appears in header between navigation controls and close button
Click download button, verify dropdown opens with three menu items visible with correct labels and icons
With menu open, click outside menu area, verify menu closes
Press 'D' key in presentation mode, verify menu toggles open/closed
Verify button styling matches close-btn (36x36px, same border, hover turns lime)
Verify menu styling: dark background, gray border, lime hover on items
